{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question is whether MLL3 is part of the ASCOM complex. In the provided documents, the only reference to the ASCOM complex is in document [11] (Kabuki Syndrome -- Pathophysiology), which states: 'Mutations of KDM6A include nonsense, frameshift, splice site, missense, large deletions, small insertions, and small deletions; a rare mosaic case of KDM6A has previously been identified. [3] This mutation is inherited in an X-linked manner; the gene is located on Xp11.23 and encodes a histone (H3)-lysine 27-specific demethylase that interacts with KMT2D in regulating gene expression in the activating signal cointegrator-2-containing complex (ASCOM). [1] [3] [5]'. This indicates that KDM6A interacts with KMT2D in the ASCOM complex. However, there is no mention of MLL3 in any of the documents. MLL3 is a related gene (a member of the KMT2 family), but the documents do not state that MLL3 is part of the ASCOM complex. Therefore, based on the available information, MLL3 is not stated to be part of the ASCOM complex.", "answer_choice": "B"}
